paper_title: Corynoneura Winnertz species from Hunan Province, Oriental China, delineated with morphological and 16S rDNA data (Diptera, Chironomidae)

methods.description: Process for collecting adult specimens from habitats near small streams and lakes, and preserving them for further molecular analyses.

steps.description: Adults were collected by light traps near the water body or swept from marginal vegetation beside natal aquatic sites.
steps.searchable_summary: collect adult specimens using light traps and sweeping from vegetation

steps.description: The specimens were preserved in 85% ethanol, and stored in the dark at 4 °C before molecular analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve specimens in ethanol and store at 4°C

methods.description: Procedure for extracting total genomic DNA from specimens and amplifying the mitochondrial 16S ribosomal RNA gene.

steps.description: Total genomic DNA of specimens was extracted from the thorax and legs using Qiagen DNA Blood & Tissue Kit.
steps.searchable_summary: extract genomic DNA using Qiagen DNA Blood & Tissue Kit

steps.description: The standard protocol of the Qiagen DNeasy Blood & Tissue Kit was used, except that the final elution volume was 100 L due to the small specimen size.
steps.searchable_summary: elute DNA with 100 L volume using Qiagen protocol

steps.description: PCR amplification of the mitochondrial 16S ribosomal RNA gene was carried out with the primers and temperature regimes given in Ekrem et al. (2010).
steps.searchable_summary: amplify 16S rRNA gene using PCR with specific primers

methods.description: Procedure for preparing and analyzing the morphological features of specimens.

steps.description: After DNA extraction, the clear exoskeleton was washed with 96% ethanol and mounted in Euparal on microscope slides together with the corresponding antennae, head, wings, and legs following the procedure outlined by Saether (1969).
steps.searchable_summary: mount exoskeleton and body parts on slides using Euparal

steps.description: Morphological nomenclature follows Saether (1980). Measurements are given as ranges followed by the mean, when three or more specimens were measured.
